issue_title: Reevaluate OpenFile workaround
issue_body:
The code includes a specific workaround for opening files on Windows 9x (Microsoft Windows 95, 98, 98 SE und ME).
Reported by this bug: http://sourceforge.net/tracker/index.php?func=detail&aid=1489454&group_id=92314&atid=600306
We should reevaluate if the code is still necessary for Java 8 support.
See https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/master/src/main/java/net/sf/jabref/util/Util.java#L484
commit_message: Fixes #95 by removing support for windows 95 and windows 98.

issue_title: Dragging entry into group
issue_body: When I drag an entry into a group, the group window jumps to the top.
commit_message: Fix #171: Dragging an entry to a group preserves scrolling

issue_title: Error setting bibtex field
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Choose an arbitrary non-multiline field
2. Enter `text` and line breaks AND an EMPTY line
3. Save database

Error: 
`Error setting field: bad position`
commit_message: Check length of text before setting caret position - fixes #185

issue_title: Bibtex writer should not save line breaks for non-multiline fields
issue_body:
Currently all whitespaces and line breaks are saved for every field.
See also #114 .
commit_message:
Reuse FieldContentParser during serialization to remove newlines from non-multiline fields.

Fixes #186

commit_message: Extracted native Windows logic and fixed #194

issue_title: Windows Position in Multi-Monitor environment
issue_body:
Does not work in multi-monitor environment. Maybe evn every environment. Windows are placed outside the monitor on the left.
See `JabRefFrame.java` starting on line 566.

commit_message: Fix #244 Only load PinToTaskbar if Windows >= 7

issue_title: GUI flashes when resizing
issue_body:
In its current state on master, the GUI window of JabRef flashes when resizing the window. This effect is quite annoying (at least to my eye).  It's hard to capture the flash in screenshot, but to reproduce it, it is sufficient to start up the GUI and resize the window by dragging at its borders.

OS: Win 7
JRE: Oracle 1.8.0_60
commit_message: Fixes #271

issue_title: JabRef help dialog has horizontal scrollbars
issue_body: Probably caused by including the license files.
commit_message: increase width of help dialog - fixes #279

issue_title: Cleanup entries not fully working
issue_body:
When running Cleanup entries, I get the error:

Nov 02, 2015 10:48:07 PM net.sf.jabref.gui.BasePanel runCommand
SEVERE: runCommand error: null

Some of the cleanup actions are performed (not sure if all are), but the status bar is not updated with the "XX entries needed a cleanup" (or whatever it says), so something breaks before that.

Guess: page number change?
commit_message: Fix #284 Do not cleanup if field is not set

issue_title: Clicking on links in About dialog
issue_body:
Two in one, but probably related (and might be updated with more information tomorrow).

When clicking on a link the following happens (except that it opens in the web browser as expected):

On my Linux computer at the office (started using `./gradlew run`): a list with all html-files are shown in the About-dialog (like a directory listing)

On my Windows laptop (started from Eclipse, no developers, authors, or licenseInformation shown): error message such as
    Nov 02, 2015 10:54:12 PM net.sf.jabref.gui.help.HelpContent setPage
    SEVERE: Could not find html-help for file 'gpl-2.0'.
    Nov 02, 2015 10:54:45 PM net.sf.jabref.gui.help.HelpContent setPage
    SEVERE: Could not find html-help for file 'external-libraries.txt'.
are shown in the console, but the about-page is OK.
commit_message: Correct handling of external files - fixes #285

issue_title: Normalize to Bibtex name format
issue_body:
Rightclick formatter in authors field goes wrong.

Minimal example:

```
Stefan Kolb and J{\"o}rg Lenhard and Guido Wirtz
```

turns out to be

```
Kolb, Stefan and J{\"o}rg Lenhard and Wirtz, Guido
```

but should be

```
Kolb, Stefan and Lenhard, J{\"o}rg and Wirtz, Guido
```
- [x] `NameListNormalizer` should be transformed to a Formatter on that occasion.

On another note: is this really THE BibTex name format? This should help with multiple firstnames?!
commit_message:
Replace NormalizeNamesFormatter by AuthorList (#1149)

and imporve the AuthorListParser to recognize colons as names separator

issue_title: UTF-8 instead of UTF8
issue_body: As discussed in #155, we switched from `UTF8` to `UTF-8`. In `net.sf.jabref.importer.OpenDatabaseActionTest.defaultEncoding`, however, `UTF8` is used. When changing that to `UTF-8`, tests fail. I think, that string should also be `UTF-8` and the test not failing.
commit_message:
Change representation of encodings to Charset

Instead of representing an encoding as a string we now use a Charset.
This idea was already discussed in PR #370.

issue_title: Preserve capital Umlauts during cleanup
issue_body: Ägypten in title field results in  {\"{A}}gypten. This should be the correct syntax, but the result of the pdf is ägypten (field title only!). {{\"{A}}}gypten produces Ägypten. This might be a bibtex bug, because the author field works without additional braces.
commit_message: Replaced regular expressions with plain strings in HTML/Unicode conversion map and fixed #405

issue_title: Unneeded double bracket required in check integrity
issue_body:
Just started using 3.0 Very good! Thanks for your work!
Just a small thing.
I'm running in Biblatex mode, but "Check integrity" is complaining that "large capitals are not masked using curly brackets {}".
But for what I know Biblatex/biber have made this requirement obsolete. That is, capital letter are preserved without any special quoting.
Thanks for considering!
mario
commit_message: Biblatex preserves capital letters, checking whether letters may be converted to lowercase within the Integrity Check action is obsolete. Fixes #412

issue_title: No program associated for help link
issue_body:
JR 3.0
Help > Help contents then I clicked on BibTex help (but it happens with all entries). An error popup appears about:
There is no program associated to perform the requested action...
With the title:
jar:file:<install dir>/JabRef-3.0.jar!/help/en/BibtexHelp.html
It suggests to go to the default programs and set the program for .html (I suppose), but the association obviously already exists.
Thanks for looking!
mario
commit_message: Fix #413 following link in jar builds

issue_title: Complains for missing "editor" for "Online" entries
issue_body:
JR 3.0 but also 2.11
Running in biblatex mode.
My "Online" entries are marked as problematic because there is no "editor" field.
But per biblatex documentation (2.1.1 page 10) you need author or editor, but both are not required.
And I have the "author" field filled.
Again, it is a small thing.
Thanks for looking!
mario
commit_message: Resolves #414 Rework BibLatex entry types

issue_title: Grouping: Switching from one group to another does not update table
issue_body:
Main table is not updated on selection of another group.

Workaround for users: Show "All entries" first before selecting the other group.
commit_message: Fix #417 (again)

issue_title: JabRef installation folder name
issue_body: Currently switched to lowercase `jabref` but was `JabRef`. I think we should revert to the old folder location?!
commit_message: Resolves #434 Revert to old JabRef installation folder name instead of jabref

issue_title: Sciencedirect fetcher non OpenAccess PDF links don't work
issue_body:
I've just tested the newly implemented "Full text article download" function, however for several articles it downloaded a version from ResearchGate but I want to have the one from Elsevier. Dependent who you are working for, you have special access privileges on certain publisher sites where you download papers.

It would be handy to specify (in the preferences) which publisher the download pdf function should prefer. Perhaps even define a priority list, e.g. Sciencedirect (Elsevier), Wiley, ResearchGate, google, etc..
commit_message: Fix #435 Retrieve non open access PDFs via HTTP DOM

issue_title: New Search bar partly hidden in Ubuntu
issue_body:
Hey,
just tried the new version.  Looks quite good! However, the new search bar at the top of the table is partly hidden on my Ubuntu 14.04
- I can see the search field, but not the optional buttons below.
- I tried with other look& feels, but this doen't change the problem. 

Unfortunately, I can't change the size of the search bar.
![bildschirmfoto vom 2015-12-01 15 08 22](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/8830305/11502836/6826fa5a-983d-11e5-99f1-4b365ee5adc4.png)
commit_message: Fixes #437: The toolbar after the search field is now correctly wrapped when using a small resolution for JabRef

issue_title: Import/Export to external database fails for PostgreSQL in 3.0 of jabref
issue_body:
Hi,

Just tried 3.0.  The import and export features for PostgreSQL fail with the following messages:

For Export - "Could not export to SQL database for the following reason:
                     This connection has been closed"

For Import - "Could not import from SQL database for the following reason:
                      This ResultSet is closed."

Establishing a connection to the database returns successfully.

Thanks

Fred
commit_message:
Create tests to fix #473 (#1269)

* Fix #473
* Remove MySQL and PostgreSQL file export
* obsolete keys are printed in exception message

issue_title: Entry preview parses html incorrectly after search with regular expression enabled
issue_body:
Jabref 3.0 as well as recent development versions do not parse the html code in the preview window correctly. This seems to happen if there is some html code WITHIN a certain bibtexfield. The citation is parsed correctly, but the "abstract" field in the preview window, which contains some autogenerated and manually inserted html code, seems to delete the "<" of each tag.
the string automatically inserted string "br>", appears the same way as my manual added italics "i>"

![bildschirmfoto vom 2015-12-17 14 16 09](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/8830305/11870525/ceb1e1da-a4c8-11e5-864b-27c0cf9bd854.png)
![bildschirmfoto vom 2015-12-17 14 16 33](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/8830305/11870526/cebd3e9a-a4c8-11e5-967d-eb8eae2e932f.png)
commit_message: Fixed #533: Preview parsed incorrectly when regular expression was enabled

I have configured "Cite command" to be `\citep`, but TeXstudio seems to ignore this setting.

![grabbed_20151221-235016](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1366654/11943376/b6fe7c4c-a83d-11e5-988a-e17c7a962ff7.png)

I assume that it is impossible to set a cite command for TeXstudio, is it?
commit_message: Fixed #564: Cite command changes are immediately reflected in the push-to-application actions, and not only after restart

issue_title: Arrow to sort field
issue_body: On 3.1 version, at least on my system, I lost the little arrow in header of field to show which field is sorted and its order (up or down). I used Ubuntu 14.04. I try GTK and MetalLookandFeel apparences.
commit_message: fixes #609 - extract tableHeader and use it later to reenable sort arrows

issue_title: Ver 3.1 comment not written correctly.
issue_body:
I have a file started with bibtex item, e.g.,

1  @ARTICLE{XXXXXX,
2      author = {XXXXXXXX},
3  .........
4  }

when I use 3.1 to save the database, one line break between encoding comment and items is missing, e.g.,

1  % Encoding: UTF-8@ARTICLE{XXXXXX,
2      author = {XXXXXXXX},
3  .........
4  }

please fix it.
commit_message:
Fix #621 and #669, along with some smaller changes to serialization

Also adds a lot of tests for serialization (and a few to the parser)

issue_title: Path vanishes if database is changed
issue_body:
Before:

![grabbed_20160102-154207](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1366654/12074779/63dca628-b167-11e5-8669-5acf5eac5bb3.png)

After:

![grabbed_20160102-154229](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/1366654/12074780/71a48d02-b167-11e5-892d-b3f343a34719.png)

Is it possible to just add the asterisk behind without removing the prefix?

Refs  #351
commit_message: Fix #622 Update all tab titles when database is changed

issue_title: Auto-completion is suggested when irrelevant
issue_body:
In general the auto-completion function is very usefull, however, sometimes it suggests phrases when not needed:

For example:
A bibtexentry fetched with google scholar gives the following Journal title:
"Zeitschrift f{\"u}r Soziologie"

I want to change it to:
"Zeitschrift für Soziologie"
-> move cursor after "}", delete the latex expression and press "ü" -> now the auto-completion suggests some other (already used) Journal name, and I have to refuse the suggestion first, to modify the entry the way I want it to be. Note, when modifying several characters, this can be pretty annoying, because I have to refuse the suggestion each time.

Expected behavior:
- auto-completion should only be suggested when the cursor is at EOL (or may be before a space character).
commit_message: Fix #644 Only suggest auto completion at the end of a word

issue_title: Author parser has problem with latex symbols
issue_body: The author `H{'e}l\grave{e}ne Fiaux` is shown as `Fiaux, H'e.` in entry preview. Would have expected `Fiaux, H`.
commit_message: Fix #651: Improve parsing of author names containing braces (#1443)

issue_title: Small icon for files in Windows 10
issue_body:
For some reason the small icon is used even if the bib file is displayed bigger. See here.
![bug](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5037600/12209705/2e584dd4-b655-11e5-9a43-ecd97e950689.png)
commit_message: Fix #655 Add new OS icons in all required sizes and dephts for Win and Mac

issue_title: hash/pound sign causes URL link to fail
issue_body:
Entering a URL into the URL field.  If the URL has a # in it, then when on the main table page, and clicking the url/doi link, the # is translated into %23 (or something like) and the URL connection fails.

Using Jabref 3.1 on Linux Mint.  Everything up to date.
commit_message: Fix #667 Do not sanitize URL before opening

issue_title: Searching for multi-word keywords fails
issue_body:
First off: thanks so much to everyone who has worked on JabRef.  It's an awesome tool!

If I do a search like `keywords=building` then this works fine.  But I cannot seem to use multi-word search terms.  None of the following searches return any results, despite the fact that I definitely have a bunch of entries which have `building management` as one of their keywords:
- `keywords="building management"`
- `keywords='building management'`
- `keywords=building management`
- `keywords=(building management)`
- `keywords=\"building management\"`
- `keywords=\'building management\'`

If I enclose even single-word search terms in quotes then this also fails.  e.g. `keywords="building"` or `keywords='building'`.

The best I can do is search for `"building management"` (i.e. exclude `keywords=`) but, of course, this returns results where `building management` is in _any_ field.

I'm using JabRef 3.2 on Ubuntu 15.10 x64 and OpenJRE 8.
commit_message: Fixes #670: Expressions using enclosed quotes (keywords="one two") did not work.

issue_title: Jabref doesn't remember import location
issue_body: The latest 3.x version has one annoying bug, or maybe I didn't explore all the preferences. Every time I **import an entry file into my current database**, Jabref starts from my main file directory where I store my huge bib database and pdfs, but the file I want to import is in "Downloads", and Jabref won't remember this location the next time I want to import a new entry. I looked into the options and find no way to change it. I remember that in 2.x version, Jabref could remember this location.
commit_message: Fix #671 Use last import working directory instead of global working directory

issue_title: Synchronize Files - key binding does not work
issue_body:
Since 3.x version, a useful functionality stops to work. Before, when I use CTRL+F4 to sync files, it normally founds and ask if I want to delete invalid links. I was unable to reproduce this behavior in the new version.
Thanks.
commit_message: Fixed #680 Synchronize Files key binding works again.

issue_title: ISBN to BibTeX Fetch updates
issue_body:
Issues  that seem left over from http://sourceforge.net/p/jabref/bugs/1241/

I tried 0-444-41881-4 and  0444418814 which return  an “Invalid ISBN” message. It is a valid ISBN and all other ISBN sites find the book. The message is misleading and should be “ISBN metadata unavailable”. Perhaps unlike http://manas.tungare.name/software/isbn-to-bibtex/  http://www.ebook.de/ does not accept  the old 10 digit ISBN. I tried 9780444418814 (the 13 digit converted ISBN) and ebook.de returned the same message. SOMETHING SHOULD BE ADDED TO THE HELP FILE TO EXPLAIN THIS MISLEADING “Invalid ISBN” MESSAGE. 

Trying to figure out why I read http://jabref.sourceforge.net/faq.php "Q: I have an ISBN. Is it possible to create an entry directly out of the ISBN?" and http://jabref.sourceforge.net/help/ISBNtoBibTeXHelp.php which are still to reflect what has changed, unlike the JabRef Help src/main/resources/help/en/ISBNtoBibTeXHelp.html (It is good that the embedded help is current). PLEASE UPDATE THE ONLINE CONTENT.
commit_message: Include https://bibtex.chimbori.com/ as fallback for the ebook.de fetcher (#2374)

issue_title: NPE when closing JabRef with new unsaved database
issue_body:
Create a new database, possibly add some entries. Close JabRef with the tab active and ignore saving it.

```
Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NullPointerException
    at java.util.prefs.AbstractPreferences.put(Unknown Source)
    at net.sf.jabref.JabRefPreferences.put(JabRefPreferences.java:915)
    at net.sf.jabref.logic.preferences.LastFocusedTabPreferences.setLastFocusedTab(LastFocusedTabPreferences.java:39)
    at net.sf.jabref.gui.JabRefFrame.tearDownJabRef(JabRefFrame.java:799)
    at net.sf.jabref.gui.JabRefFrame.quit(JabRefFrame.java:899)
```

should be quite easy to fix. The main problem is what should happen? Should one just reset the last focused tab preference or leave it as is (which doesn't make sense as one might have closed that tab).
commit_message: Fixed #687 Fixed NPE when closing JabRef with new unsaved database.

issue_title: Headers in main table are lowercase
issue_body: Some headers in the main table are shown in lowercase while others (like Ranking) are in uppercase. (Version: current master)
commit_message: Resolves #754 Headers in main table are lowercase by default

issue_title: dynamically group, free-form search
issue_body:
The 'Dynamically group entries by a free-form search expression' doesn't work if a search expression is added without any field name.

For example, I have a Dynamic Group called _Reviews_, and the group is set to 'Dynamically group entries by a free-form search expression' with a Search expression: _review_. In the description is says: "This search contains entries in which any field contains the term **review** (case insensitive). Hint: To search..." However, there are no entries visible when I press OK. But, if I type _keywords=review_ in the Search expression then in the description it says: "This search contains entries in which the field **keywords** contains the term **review.** The search is case insensitive." and I see a bunch of entries when I press OK.

This works as intended in Jabref 2.10, but not in Jabref 3.2.
commit_message: Fixed #803 dynamic group using free-form search returned wrong results

issue_title: Curly braces ignored in in-text citations
issue_body:
In the reference,

```
@Manual{JabRef_software,
  Title                    = {JabRef},
  Author                   = {{JabRef Development Team}},
  Year                     = {2015},
  Version                  = {Version 2.11.1},
  Url                      = {http://www.jabref.org}
}
```

The curly braces in the Author field are ignored, causing, for example, its associated in-text citation to be presented as "(Team, 2015)", rather than, "(JabRef Development Team, 2015)". Curly braces should be honored in in-text citations.
commit_message:
Fixed #856: Clicking on browse for manual OpenOffice does now work
Fixed #815: Curly braces in OO/LO citation no longer ignored
- Improved the handling of curly braces in StringUtil method
- Added new test cases
- Code cleanup: Replaced ActionListener with Lamdbas

issue_title: OSX - Exception when adding the icon to the dock
issue_body: The OSX installer issues a NPE when adding the icon to the dock
commit_message: fixed addToDock functionality during the install process for OSX - closes #822

issue_title: MimeTypeDetector fails for local URIs
issue_body:
When a URL encoded local URI is passed to the function

`MimeTypeDetector.isPdfContentType(String url)`

the return value turns out to be False if `url` is a local URI. 
If one likes to implement `FullTextFinder` using a temporary copy of some fetched PDF this needs to be changed.
commit_message: Fixes #824 Mime type detector can now also handle local file links [ci skip]

issue_title: NPE when clearing search bar
issue_body:
The following is called twice in `SearchBar`:

`searchQueryHighlightObservable.fireSearchlistenerEvent(null);`

which is a bit unfortunate since the corresponding method begins:

``` java
    public void fireSearchlistenerEvent(SearchQuery searchQuery) {
        Objects.requireNonNull(searchQuery);
```

not sure what makes sense to replace the null with though...
commit_message:
Fixes #838 which caused a NPE when resetting the search.
A changelog entry is omitted as the bug was never in a release.

issue_title: OpenOffice Manual connect  - Clicking on browse does nothing
issue_body:
While setting up my Libre Ofifce connection around for #815 I noticed that Browse does nothing.
However, directly inserting the correct path works.

There is also some odd behaviour with Autodetection.
When I chose the correct path for Autodection it displays an error message " Autodection failed", but it still connects.

Fixed #815: Curly braces in OO/LO citation no longer ignored

issue_title: NPE during cleanup -> PDF renaming
issue_body:
JabRef 2016-02-28--master--a0444ab

I have following bib entry:

```
@Article{MLU2011,
  author =        {Ralph Mietzner and Frank Leymann and Tobias Unger},
  title =         {Horizontal and vertical combination of multi-tenancy patterns in service-oriented applications},
  journal =       {Enterprise Information Systems},
  year =          {2011},
  volume =        {5},
  number =        {1},
  pages =         {59--77},
  month =         feb,
  doi =           {10.1080/17517575.2010.492950},
  file =          {:Veröffentlichungen\\ART-2010-06 - Patterns Journal Version.pdf:PDF},
  publisher =     {Informa {UK} Limited}
}
```

(The wrong indent is solved by #851)

I do a cleanup on that entry with "Rename PDFs to given filename format pattern" only. The file does not get renamed, but a NPE is risen.

Side comment: The status box still shows "Doing a cleanup for entries..." and does not show "Error during cleanup" (or "Error" or something similar).

```

    at net.sf.jabref.util.Util.getLinkedFileName(Util.java:207) ~[JabRef-dev-fat.jar:?]
    at net.sf.jabref.logic.cleanup.RenamePdfCleanup.cleanup(RenamePdfCleanup.java:66) ~[JabRef-dev-fat.jar:?]
    at net.sf.jabref.logic.cleanup.CleanupWorker.cleanup(CleanupWorker.java:64) ~[JabRef-dev-fat.jar:?]
    at net.sf.jabref.gui.actions.CleanupAction.doCleanup(CleanupAction.java:161) ~[JabRef-dev-fat.jar:?]
    at net.sf.jabref.gui.actions.CleanupAction.run(CleanupAction.java:106) ~[JabRef-dev-fat.jar:?]
    at sun.reflect.GeneratedMethodAccessor23.invoke(Unknown Source) ~[?:?]
    at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(Unknown Source) ~[?:1.8.0_66]
    at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Unknown Source) ~[?:1.8.0_66]
    at spin.Invocation.evaluate(Invocation.java:175) ~[JabRef-dev-fat.jar:?]
```

issue_title: Quantifying spaces in regex results in illegal search expression
issue_body:
- JabRef version (available in the About box): 3.3dev snapshot 29/02/2016
- Operating system and version: Ubuntu 15.10 Wily
- Steps to reproduce:
  Quantifying spaces in regex doesn't work. It used to work. All my regex filter are not functionning anymore (and the groups are empty  because the regex is seen as faulty). I do not know what version caused this, however, I suspect it was fine in the version 2.
  1. Type the following regexes in either the search field or a filter regex field. All should work :
     - `a[^b]` : a followed by not b
     - `ab+`: a followed by at least one b
     - `ab*`: a followed by any number of b
     - `ab?`: a optionally followed by a b
     - `a`: a followed by a space
     - `(ab)`: ab in a group
  2. Those won't work and give a `Search failed: illegal search expression`:
     - `a[^ ]`: a followed by not space
     - `a +`: a followed by at least one space
     - `a *`: a followed by any number of spaces
     - `a ?`: a optionally followed by a space
     - `(a b)`: a b in a group, with space in the middle

Is this a desired behaviour ? I frequently use `[^ ]` to designate anything but a space and frequently put in alternatives such as : `(word and paradigm)|(item and arrangment)`.
commit_message: Fixes #962 Do not split Regexes and only match on entire fields
